question: A Polar Stereographic chart is used for navigation. The straight line between A (75° 00'.0S, 166° 00'.0E) and B (78° 00'.0S, 154° 00'.0E) is drawn on this chart. The True Track angle of the rhumb line is 223°. Calculate the direction (°T) of the straight line in position B.

000. From A to B on the chart measures 3.8 cm, the distance from A to B in NM is:

The chart distance between Meridians 10° apart at latitude 65° North is 9.5 cm. The chart scale at this latitude approximates:
Which statement is correct about the scale of a Lambert projection?
options: The scale reaches its minimum value at the standard parallels.
The scale reaches its minimum value at the parallel of origin.
The scale reaches its maximum value at the parallel of origin.
The scale reaches its maximum value at the standard parallels.
true: The scale reaches its minimum value at the parallel of origin.

question: The positions A (30°00’N, 017°30’E) and B at longitude (30°00’N, 023°30’E) are plotted on a Lambert chart with a constant of the cone of 0.5. A and B are connected by a straight line. The True Track measured at A is 088.5°. What is the True Track measured at B?
23 ) On a Mercator’s projection the distance between (17°N, 035°E) and (17°N, 040°E) is 5 cm. The scale at 57°N is approximately:

From Rakovnik (50° 05.9' N, 013° 41.5' E) to Frankfurt FFM (50° 05.9' N, 008° 38.3' E) the True Track of departure along the straight line is 272.0°. The constant of the cone of this Lambert conformal projection is:
An aeronautical chart is conformal when:
options: the meridians and parallels are perpendicular to each other.
At any point the scale over a short distance in the direction of the parallel is equal to the scale in the direction of the meridian and the meridians are perpendicular to the parallels.
every great circle is represented by a straight line in the map.
the map is an equidistant normal projection.
true: At any point the scale over a short distance in the direction of the parallel is equal to the scale in the direction of the meridian and the meridians are perpendicular to the parallels.

question: On a Mercator chart, at latitude 60°N, the distance measured between W002° and E008° is 20 cm. The scale of this chart at latitude 60°N is approximately…

000. The length on the chart between 'C' N60° E008° and 'D' N60° W008° is:

How does the scale vary on a Direct Mercator chart?
options: The scale increases south of the Equator and decreases north of the Equator.
The scale increases with increasing distance from the Equator.
The scale decreases with increasing distance from the Equator.
true: The scale increases with increasing distance from the Equator.

question: On a Direct Mercator chart at latitude 15°S, a certain length represents a distance of 120 NM on the earth. The same length on the chart will represent on the earth, at latitude 10°N, a distance of:

On a Lambert conformal conic chart, the distance between parallels of latitude spaced the same number of degrees apart:
options: is larger between the standard parallels and is smaller outside them
is smaller between the standard parallels than outside them
is constant between the standard parallels and is greater outside them
true: is smaller between the standard parallels than outside them

question: What is the chart distance between longitudes 179°E and 175°W on a direct Mercator chart with a scale of 1: 5 000 000 at the Equator?
Where on a Direct Mercator projection is the chart convergency correct compared to the Earth convergency?
options: At the two parallels of tangency.

question: On a Mercator chart, the distance between positions A and B, located on the same parallel and 10° longitude apart, is 6 cm. The scale at the parallel is 1: 9 260 000.What is the latitude of A and B?
On a Direct Mercator projection, at latitude 45° North, a certain length represents 70 NM. At latitude 30° North, the same length represents approximately…

The standard parallels of a Lambert chart are 26°N and 48°N and the stated scale is 1:2 500
000. Which statement is correct?
options: The scale at 37°N is larger than the scale at 20°N.
The scale at 30°N is smaller than the scale at 37°N.
The scale at 28°N is smaller than the scale at 24°N.
The scale at 46°N is larger than the scale at 50°N.
true: The scale at 28°N is smaller than the scale at 24°N.

question: At 60°N the scale of a direct Mercator chart is 1: 3 000 000.What is the scale at the Equator?
Which statement is true about the parallel of origin of a conformal chart?
options: The parallel of origin is the parallel at which the scale reaches its minimum value.
The parallel of origin is the parallel at which the scale reaches its maximum value.
The parallel of origin is the only parallel at which the chart is conformal.
The parallel of origin together with the standard parallel(s), are the only parallels at which the chart is conformal.
true: The parallel of origin is the parallel at which the scale reaches its minimum value.

question: The total length of the 53°N parallel of latitude on a direct Mercator chart is 133 cm. What is the approximate scale of the chart at latitude 30°S?

000. What is the scale at latitude 60°S?

000. Standard parallels 36°N and 60°N. A (53°N, 010°W), B (53°N, 020°W). The distance on the map between position A and position B measured along the rhumb line:
On a Lambert conformal conic projection with two standard parallels…
options: the lines of longitude are shown as parallel straight lines.
the scale is only correct at the parallel of origin.
the scale is only correct along the standard parallels.
all great circles are shown as straight lines.
true: the scale is only correct along the standard parallels.

question: The standard parallels of a Lambert’s conical projection are 07°40’N and 38°20’N. For this chart, what is the approximate constant of the cone?
The two standard parallels of a Lambert conformal conic projection are at 10°40’N and 41°20’N. The constant of the cone for this chart is approximately…
On a Lambert Conformal Conic chart earth convergency is most accurately represented at the:
options: north and south limits of the chart

question: On a Lambert conformal conic chart the convergence of the meridians…
options: is the same as earth convergency at the parallel of origin.
varies as the secant of the latitude.
equals earth convergency at the standard parallels.
true: is the same as earth convergency at the parallel of origin.

question: Which statement is correct about the scale of a Polar Stereographic projection of the Northern polar area?
options: The scale reaches its minimum value at the equator.
The scale reaches its maximum value at the North pole.
The scale reaches its maximum value at the 45°N.
The scale reaches its minimum value at the North pole.
true: The scale reaches its minimum value at the North pole.

question: How does the chart convergency change with latitude in a Lambert Conformal projection?
options: It changes with cosine of latitude.
It is constant and does not change with latitude.
It changes with sine of latitude.
It increases with increase of latitude.
true: It is constant and does not change with latitude.

question: On a Lambert conformal conic chart, with two standard parallels, the quoted scale is correct:
options: in the area between the standard parallels

question: What is the constant of the cone for a Lambert conic projection whose standard parallels are at 50°N and 70°N?
What is the value of the convergence factor on a Polar Stereographic chart?
The scale on a Lambert conformal conic chart…
options: varies slightly as a function of latitude and longitude.
is constant along a meridian of longitude.
is constant across the whole map.
is constant along a parallel of latitude.
true: is constant along a parallel of latitude.

question: A Lambert conformal conic chart has a constant of the cone of 0.75.A straight line track is drawn from A (40°N, 050°W) to B. The initial track at A is 043°(T) and the final track at B is 055°(T). What is the longitude of B?
Which one of the following statements is correct concerning the appearance of great circles, with the exception of meridians, on a Polar Stereographic chart?
options: They are complex curves that can be convex and/or concave to the Pole.
The higher the latitude, the closer they approximate to a straight line.
Any straight line is a great circle.
They are curves convex to the Pole.
true: The higher the latitude, the closer they approximate to a straight line.

question: On a Lambert chart (Standard Parallels 37°N and 65°N), with respect to the straight line drawn on the map between A ( N49° W030°) and B (N48° W040°), the:
options: Rhumb line is to the north, the great circle is to the south
Great circle and rhumb line are to the north
Great circle is to the north, the rhumb line is to the south
Great circle and rhumb line are to the south
true: Great circle and rhumb line are to the south

question: A Lambert conformal conic chart has a constant of the cone of 0.80.  A straight line course drawn on this chart from A (53°N 004°W) to B is 080° at A; course at B is 092°(T). What is the longitude of B?

The nominal scale of a Lambert conformal conic chart is the:
options: Mean scale between the parallels of the secant cone
Mean scale between pole and equator

The chart that is generally used for navigation in polar areas is based on a:
A rhumb line on a Direct Mercator chart appears as a…
options: curve convex to the nearer pole.
small circle concave to the nearer pole.

question: On which of the following chart projections is it NOT possible to represent the north or South Poles?
On a Direct Mercator chart, apart from meridians and the Equator, a great circle will be represented by a…
On a Direct Mercator chart, a rhumb line appears as a:
options: Small circle concave to the nearer pole
Curve convex to the nearer pole

question: On a Direct Mercator chart, meridians are…
options: inclined, equally spaced, straight lines that meet at the nearer pole.
parallel, unequally spaced, vertical straight lines.
parallel, equally spaced, vertical straight lines.
inclined, unequally spaced, curved lines that meet at the nearer pole.
true: parallel, equally spaced, vertical straight lines.

question: Select the correct statement regarding great circles on a Direct Mercator chart.
options: They approximate to straight lines between the standard parallels.
They are all curves convex to the Equator.
With the exception of meridians and the Equator, they are curves concave to the Equator.
They are all curves concave to the Equator.
true: With the exception of meridians and the Equator, they are curves concave to the Equator.

question: Parallels of latitude on a Direct Mercator chart are:
options: straight lines converging above the pole
arcs of concentric circles equally spaced

question: On a Direct Mercator, rhumb lines are…

A straight line on a Lambert Conformal Projection chart for normal flight planning purposes:
options: can only be a parallel of latitude

question: On a Lambert Conformal Conic chart great circles that are not meridians are:
options: curves concave to the pole of projection
curves concave to the parallel of origin
straight lines within the standard parallels
true: curves concave to the parallel of origin

question: Which one of the following describes the appearance of rhumb lines, except meridians, on a Polar Stereographic chart?

Two places are situated on the same parallel in the Southern Hemisphere. The great circle, rhumb line and the straight line between these places are drawn on a Polar Stereographic Projection. Which statement is correct?
options: The great circle is situated between the parallel and the straight line, because the concave side of the great circle is always pointed towards the equator.
The rhumb line is situated between the great circle and the straight line because the shortest distance between to places on Earth is the great circle.
The great circle is situated between the parallel and the straight line, because the concave side of the great circle is always pointed towards the pole.
The correct sequence from North to South is: Great circle, straight line, rhumb line.
true: The great circle is situated between the parallel and the straight line, because the concave side of the great circle is always pointed towards the pole.

An aircraft is flying a track of 255°(M). At 22:54 UTC, it crosses radial 360° from a VOR station. At 23:00 UTC, it crosses radial 330° from the same station. What is the distance between the aircraft and the station at 23:00 UTC?

Randomly different to what it was at 22:54 UTC.
Less than it was at 22:54 UTC.
Greater than it was at 22:54 UTC.
true: The same as it was at 22:54 UTC

question: Where is the parallel of origin for a Direct Mercator projection?
options: Where the convergency factor is zero.

Which of the following statements most accurately describes a great circle route on a Lambert conformal conic chart? A straight line will approximate to a great circle course if the…
options: straight line is close to the parallel of origin.
great circle course is a curve concave to the parallel of origin.
straight line follows the parallel of origin.
sine of the parallel of origin is equal to the convergence factor.
true: straight line is close to the parallel of origin.

question: (For this question See Image, a Lamberts Conformal chart of the northern hemisphere). Where is the parallel of origin?

On a Lambert conformal projection the scale at a point at 60°N in the direction of True North is 1:1 000
000. What is the scale at the same point in an easterly direction?
options: The scale in this direction cannot be calculated as the longitude of the position is not given.

question: The Lambert conformal conic projection has to be produced mathematically to:
options: Display the great circle as a straight line.
Obtain an equal scale between the standard parallels.
Display the meridians as straight lines.

question: What can be said about the area represented on a Lambert projection which lies between the two standard parallels of the chart?
options: In this area the scale of the chart differs less than 1% from the stated scale of the chart.
Only in this area is the chart conformal.
The True Track of departure and the True Track of arrival between two positions situated in this area are practically equal.
In this area the rhumb line and great circle between two positions situated in this area practically coincide.
true: In this area the scale of the chart differs less than 1% from the stated scale of the chart.

question:
(For this question see image). Which of the diagrams most accurately represents the great circle route from A to B on a Lambert conformal conic chart?

220) °On a Lambert conformal projection the chart convergency between two positions depends on:
options: the difference in longitude and the mean latitude of the two positions.
the latitude of the parallel of origin only.
the difference in longitude of the two positions and the latitude of the parallel of origin.
the difference in longitude of the two positions only.
true: the difference in longitude of the two positions and the latitude of the parallel of origin.

question: The scale of a Lambert conformal conic chart is the:
options: mean scale between the parallels of the secant cone
mean scale between pole and equator
